methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $34M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Terrahaptix is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Nathan Nwachuku and Maxwell Maduka that has raised $34M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private peers and 2 public companies operating in the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $34M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $68M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $125M (NODA AI) to $500M (Stark Defence). The peer set shows material dispersion, reflecting heterogeneity in stage maturity, technology differentiation, and commercial traction within the Defense & Security frontier-tech segment.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $58M to $259M, with a median of $146M. The stage-progression and peer-median methods diverge materially—reflecting tension between raising-stage mechanics and market comparables. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, technology defensibility relative to the peer set, and the timing and certainty of next-round capital. The wide peer range ($125M–$500M) underscores sector-specific risk around commercialization velocity and end-customer acquisition in defense contracting.
